© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Key Takeaways:
• This lesson is about creating your first Prelaunch Content
• Note: it does NOT have to be video-based… I’m going to use the word “video”
because most launches are video-based these days.
• PLC1 is about the opportunity – the journey… showing your avatar the opportunity
to change their lives for the better.
• PLC1 is the opportunity, PLC2 is the transformation, and PLC3 is the ownership
experience.
• One of the things that's really important is understanding who your avatar is, who
your prospect is, what their pain points are, what their desires are, what keeps
them up at night. What are the dangers in their lives?
• You must understand your avatar and answer the “why” question - why they
should care? (The why is because of the opportunity for them to have a dramatic
change in their life.)
• You have to hit on positioning – why they should listen to you?
• Then you need to teach valuable content.
• You need to address objections – you don’t need to answer every single one of
them, but you definitely need to raise and answer the top one or two.
• Foreshadow what’s coming in PLC2.
• End your video with a call to action.
• Download the PLC1 Quickstart Guide under the video for sample language to use,
what to avoid, and a PLC1 checklist.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Okay, let's talk about PLC1, or Prelaunch Content number 1.
Now, as I stressed over and over, your launch does not have to be video-based.
You could do it any number of ways, but these days, most launches do tend to be video-
based, whether they're direct-to-camera, talking-head style, or whether they're screen
capture, and this is a screen capture video. They could be either one of those, or they
could be something else. They could be email, they could be blog posts, they could be
podcasts, whatever, but in general, I'm going to use the word "video" because most
launches are video-based these days.
Video 1, PLC1 is about the opportunity. I also consider this the journey, where
you're showing them the opportunity, "them" being your avatar, you're showing them the
opportunity to change their lives for the better, to increase the pleasure or decrease the
pain, to learn to play guitar songs, or to learn how to quit smoking. It's the opportunity
for them to change their lives. It could also be the journey that they go through as they
change their lives. That's what PLC1 is all about.
I'm going to show this to you over and over. You start with that Pre-Prelaunch,
you move into PLC1, "The Opportunity," PLC2, which is "The Transformation," PLC3,
"The Ownership Experience," and then eventually the Sales Page (the Sales Video, the
Sales Letter). PLC1, 2, and 3… I also like to look at this as the "why," PLC1 is the "why,"
PLC2 is the "what," and PLC3 is the "how to." That's another way to think about this.
Walking forward through here, we have those three pieces of content. One of the
things that's really important is understanding who your avatar is, who your prospect is,
what their pain points are, what their desires are, what keeps them up at night. What are
the dangers in their lives? What are the big opportunities in their lives? What do they
really want? What are their hot points? What are their top fears and frustrations? What
do they really want, what do they desire?
Often, a big part of the story is how you found the solution that you're about to
share, how you were able to eliminate those frustrations or achieve those desires, and
why have you decided to share it.
Now, for me, in sharing Product Launch Formula, a lot of this is tied to my story,
because I had this issue. I didn't have a business, I needed to create a business, then after
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
I had a business started, I needed to figure out how to sell, and how to sell in a, you
know, in an honest way, with integrity, with congruence. It very much was my story.
There's a chance that your story might not be your avatar story.
For example, I had someone who came to me, they were a psychologist, and they
were helping people with problems that they never had. That's fine; you can do that. You
can tell the story of your clients, and the successes and transformations that they've had.
It doesn't have to be your story, but often, with my Product Launch Formula Owners, I've
noticed that it is about their story that they're sharing.
Okay, walking forward here in this document. So, the overview is that this is
about the "why." You really need to understand your prospect and what they're looking
for, and what they're moving towards, and what they're moving away from, and then you
must, you have to answer that "why" question. Why should they care? Why should they
spend their time paying attention to you? The "why" is because of this opportunity for
them to have a dramatic change in their life.
Even if it's learning to hit a better serve in your tennis game, it's still a
transformation of something that they're interested in, or they're passionate about. It
could be that that guitar that's sitting in their corner, they finally learn to play a full song
on the guitar, or it could be something like I mentioned, they learn to quit smoking, and
it makes a dramatic change not just in their life, but in their children's life, and their
grandchildren's life, and the rest of their family's life. This is "why should they care?"
And to show them why they should care, you have to show what that outcome is, where
that journey's taking them to, what ... The opportunity for them to have a better life with
more fun, or more fulfillment, or less pain.
Okay, walking forward into the framework, this is just a general recipe for PLC1.
You want to show that opportunity, show how their life is going to positively change,
show what it could look like, what their life could look like.
Again, with Product Launch Formula, I talk about the before and after, what my
life was like before and what it's like after. Before I had a business, before I had a good
income, before I had freedom in my life, and then I talk about it after, and I can talk
about my Product Launch Formula Owners over the years, and the transformation in
their life.
That's the opportunity, to have this vastly different life, to have a vastly greater
income, to have a greater impact on the world, to be able to share your work with the
world and help people, to be able to have freedom of time, freedom of who you hang out

5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
with, the freedom of who you work with, the freedom of where you live. That's the
opportunity that I'm sharing. The opportunity you'll share will be very different, but you
have to think about what that opportunity is, and then show and tell how their life is
going to change.
Number two, there's positioning. What is your positioning? Why should they
listen to you? Well, a lot of times people look at me and they say, "Jeff, you've got this
amazing positioning," but the reality is, I didn't have it when I started. You can, just by
starting to publish and putting out great Prelaunch content, that will show your
positioning.
In this module, we'll show you some PLC that some of our students have shown,
and you can see how they create positioning in their life.
Then number three, there's "teach." It's important that you just don't go on and
on and on and on about the opportunity; people will get sick of that. You can open up,
you can share that opportunity, but then you do move to teaching. Again, just using
myself as an example, in my first video, I definitely build this opportunity, I build this
positioning, but then I actually go in and I teach the Sideways Sales Letter, and I teach it
to a very deep degree.
Then number four, you always want ... This is a thing that a lot of people miss, is
that no matter what you're selling, there's going to be objections. People are going to
have objections to what you're talking about, to objections to how their life can change,
to whether it will work for them, to whether it will work in their situation, and you need
to talk about those objections. You can't just put your head in the sand and pretend they
don't exist, because there's always going to be a number of objections. There's usually
going to be two to three really big ones, and then a bunch of smaller ones, and you don't
need to raise and answer every single one of them, but you definitely need to raise and
answer the top one or two.
Here's a hint: Just because you answer them in this video doesn't mean then
they're gone forever. You need to keep on coming back to them and continue to answer
them. Just don't pretend that they don't exist. Don't ignore them. That's one of the real
powers of Product Launch Formula - it gives you these three videos, plus your sales
video, plus the email copy all in between, to continually talk about these objections and
answer them. Because that's one of the most effective ways to make sales, is to remove
the objections.
Then, you want to foreshadow what's coming in PLC2. You let your people know
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 8 --

6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
there's another video coming, and something that's cool that's going to be in it, and you
try to hook them. This is just like, TV does this really well right now, serial TV. They do a
really good job of creating these threads and letting you know, and foreshadowing that
something's coming.
And then you want to end your video with a call to action, which is usually ask for
a comment on your blog or in social media.
That's just this overall framework. You open with showing the opportunity, then
you position yourself, then you teach, raise and answer objections, foreshadow the next
video, and then call to action, get people to comment.
Here is sort of a little formula I often use, or here's some examples. It starts off
with "I'm a lot like you," or "I think we're a lot alike," "This is where I was," and when
you say that, "this is where I was," that situation you were in is a situation that ... You
want that to be a situation that a lot of people can identify, a lot of your avatar, they can
identify with where you were.
For me, when I was starting out, I had very little money, I had absolutely nothing
going on, I didn't have a business, I didn't have a list, I didn't have this expert
positioning, I didn't have a lot of people following me. I just, I was starting from zero.
Even if the people I'm talking to are not starting at zero, a lot of them are much further
advanced, but they can identify with that.
Then another powerful thing is to share why ... Basically, what you aspired to be,
where you wanted to go, and then how you discovered whatever it is that you're sharing -
the secrets. "Here's a couple secrets I learned." I mean, for me, this is very simple. When
I started out doing my first launch, I didn't know how to sell, so I accidentally, almost,
created this new formula, launch formula process, because I didn't know the traditional
way to sell, so I invented something new, and it just so happened it worked really, really
well, and I kept on doing it over and over and over and over and over, and got better at it,
and that's all part of the story that I share.
It's 100% true, it's very, very powerful, because it also aligns with a lot of people,
they don't feel comfortable with selling. They don't want to feel cheesy, they don't want
to feel slimy, they just want people to think they're cool and they're nice, and it just so
happens Product Launch Formula aligns with that, and the reason I created it, and the
way I created it, aligns with that, because I've felt those same ways. It all lines up
perfectly with what many of the folks, many of my avatar are thinking and feeling.

7
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Okay, so moving along, these are some of the things that you want to avoid. This
first bullet, it says, "Avoid mentioning your product or that you're going to be making an
offer." This is not a hard and fast rule, and I know when you're teaching, people love,
love, love hard and fast rules. If you actually look at my first PLC, I actually do mention
Product Launch Formula. I mention it a couple times. So this is one of the deals where
you can start ... This idea of seeding, which is, that's where you start to mention your
product and your offer ahead of time.
It all depends on your first video and how comfortable you feel, and how strongly
your positioning is, but you actually can mention it in PLC1 if you want. Most of the time,
especially early on in your launch career, most of my PLF Owners will avoid this, but this
is one of those where if you're advanced, advanced enough to know when you can break
the rules, then you can mention it. Clearly, I've done a lot of launches, and in my first
PLC, I do actually mention the product itself.
The bullet point, second one here is "Avoid making your videos too long." There's
this old saying, "Your marketing can never be too long, it can only be too boring." We all
tend to get a little boring when we start talking about ourselves, and talking about our
products, and talking about our business, so you have to be really, really careful here.
I think that you should, in your first launch, probably aim at 12 to 18 minutes. It's
not going to be easy. It's hard to make a really short, tight video.
Now, my first PLC is closer to 25 minutes. I've been doing this a long time, and I
work really, really hard, but don't make them longer just to make them longer. Don't say,
"Jeff did a 25-minute video, so I'm going to do a 25-minute video." If I could make that
video in 23 minutes, I would. If I could make it in 19 minutes, I would. But to fit
everything in there, I had to make it that long. I do a lot of things to keep it moving
quick, moving as quickly as I can, and make it as tight as I can. Don't make your videos
long just to make them long. Make them as short as you can and still tell the entire story.
Then this bottom one, "Avoid having any other links or distractions on your
Prelaunch page," incredibly important. When you've got them there watching your video,
watching your PLC, you don't want other links on the page that could take them other
places. You want to hold their attention. You just want to be super-focused on getting
them to consume that content.
Then we just have your checklist. I'm not going to read these off for you, but this
is just basically your checklist to go through, to make sure you hit everything in PLC1.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
